scores.correctness_notes: Gets the basic structure right (log-targets, type, location, services, labels, pebble_service auto-label) but has two notable gaps: (1) omits the required 'override' field from nearly all YAML examples, and (2) incorrectly states loki is the 'only supported type currently' when the gold standard documents opentelemetry and syslog as valid protocol types with different location formats.
scores.hallucination_notes: The Go-template label syntax ({{ .Metadata.UnitName }}, {{ .Metadata.ModelName }}, etc.) appears fabricated — Pebble uses $ENV_VAR substitution in label values, not Go templates with a Metadata object. The ops.pebble.Layer usage and LogProxyConsumer library reference appear plausible but the template syntax is a meaningful inaccuracy.
